title: Production Vision 2025
body: When the ten production vision points get treated as aspirational rather than operational, the gaps compound — poor floor levels slow dump cycles, wrong excavator position drives backloading, neglected roads cut truck speed, and the whole system underperforms in ways that are hard to trace to a single cause. The YTD waste deficit of −87.4% through May is what that compounding looks like at contract scale.
keyPoints: Volume per hour, not volume per day. A fleet that works hard for six hours and idles for six is not the same as one that works steadily for twelve. Available hours are the denominator — closing the A&U gap is the job.

Excavator positioning sets the rate for the whole fleet. Wrong direction, insufficient clearance, or no bench height forces backloading and kills truck utilisation. Get the machine positioned before you send trucks in.
Mine sequentially by stripping layers. Cherry-picking faces leaves unpredictable bench heights, congests roads, and blocks grade control from managing blend — all at the same time.

Road condition is a production input, not a maintenance task. High spots and loose material on ramps and running boards cost you truck speed and tyre life. The grader's nine-plus days below 20% utilisation in June is already showing in cycle times.
apply: With Ramp 144 on deadline tomorrow and the ISO IMS audit running on site today, check that the ramp surface is properly graded — a rough ramp under audit observation compounds two problems at once.
